mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat after prior tightening, the discount-rate channel dominates and equity valuations expand. This predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, especially when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are negative or not above unconditional average in periods where FEDFUNDS is unchanged or declining, or if the sign flips positive during clear tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market deterioration and weaker future consumption, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when PAYEMS is also slowing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE rises by >0.3pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A broad increase in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal household and business spending, supporting revenue growth and higher equity prices over the next quarter. The signal should be positive when accompanied by rising HOUST.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS does not correspond to above-median 60-day SPX returns over a rolling 3-year window, or if the sign turns negative during demand expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained gain in INDPRO with stable unemployment indicates improving productive capacity rather than overheating. That combination should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through better earnings leverage and reduced recession risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises more than 1.0% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if episodes of rising INDPRO above the trigger are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than 50%, or if the signal vanishes when UNRATE is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains high, real rate pressure rises and equity duration gets repriced lower. This should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend turns up by more than 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative after inflation re-acceleration episodes, or if the effect is absent when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in PAYEMS signals weakening payroll growth and impending demand softness, which should weigh on equities over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when UNRATE is rising at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if adding UNRATE does not improve the hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST reflects resilient housing demand and stronger cyclical activity, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved growth expectations. The effect should be most visible when GDPC1 is also expanding.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 8% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if strong HOUST surges fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal breaks down when GDPC1 growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in INDPRO alongside low unemployment indicates non-inflationary real activity improvement, supporting earnings and risk appetite. This should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, but only in expansionary regimes with no labor-market deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO with stable or improving labor data do not yield positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the relation is equally strong during labor-market deterioration.
